meta.title: Greene Family Heritage
meta.subtitle: Tracing the Greene lineage from Portarlington to Dublin and beyond

people.name: Thos Green
people.notes: Earliest known ancestor. Appears in Tithe Applotment Books for Ballyburly parish, King's County, 1830s. Approximately 8km north of Portarlington.
sources: Tithe Applotment Books, Ballyburly parish, King's County, 1830-36

people.name: Joseph Greene
people.notes: Confirmed father of Mary Bridget Greene (b.1874). Probable father of Thomas Greene (b.~1871). Married Elizabeth Foran. May be connected to Lucy Greene (widow in Kilmalogue, 1901 census).
sources: Ancestry record for Mary Bridget Greene (b.1874, d.1966, Queens NY)
Civil birth records pending verification on IrishGenealogy.ie

people.name: Elizabeth Foran
people.notes: Wife of Joseph Greene. Mother of Mary Bridget Greene (b.1874, Portarlington). Maiden name Foran.
sources: Ancestry record for Mary Bridget Greene

people.name: Lucy Greene
people.notes: Widow by 1901, living in Kilmalogue, Portarlington North, King's Co. Possibly Joseph Greene's widow or a relative. Had daughter Lizzie who married Christopher Gannon. Absent from 1911 census (likely deceased).
sources: 1901 Census: Kilmalogue, Portarlington North, King's Co., House 16

people.name: Thomas Christopher Greene
people.occupation: Shop Porter (1901), Gentleman's Club Steward (1911), Warehouse Porter (1951)
people.notes: Central figure of this research. Full name Thomas Christopher Greene (discovered from Ancestry marriage index). Born c.1870 on the Offaly side of Portarlington (age 81 at death in 1951). Moved to Dublin by 1899. Lied about his age to enlist in the British Army at around age 14 (~1884) — likely the Prince of Wales's Leinster Regiment (Royal Canadians), headquartered at Birr Barracks, ~25 miles from Portarlington. Underage enlistment was common in the late Victorian army. May have served during the Boer War (1899-1902). Married twice: first to Elizabeth (d. c.1912-1925), then to Bridget Clarke. Marriage registered Dublin South, 1899, Vol 2, Page 593. Died 6 December 1951 at 38 Goldenbridge Avenue, Inchicore, Dublin, age 81. Cause of death: carcinoma of stomach, cachexia, cardiac failure. Death registered by his son John Greene, who was living at the same address. Still listed as 'Warehouse Porter' at death — a working man to the end.
sources: 1901 Census: Ranelagh Avenue, Rathmines & Rathgar East, Dublin (ID 1293881)
1911 Census: Clonliffe Avenue, Mountjoy, Dublin (ID 15408)
Death Certificate: Entry 486, 6 December 1951, registered Dublin South, Registrar M. Ryan

people.name: Elizabeth Greene (née unknown)
people.notes: First wife of Thomas Greene. Seven years older than Thomas. Married c.1899 (12 years married in 1911 census). Three children born, three living in 1911. Maiden name unknown — her marriage record on IrishGenealogy.ie would reveal it.
sources: 1901 Census: Ranelagh Avenue, Rathmines, Dublin
1911 Census: Clonliffe Avenue, Mountjoy, Dublin — 12 years married, 3 born, 3 living

people.name: Bridget Greene (née Clarke)
people.notes: Second wife of Thomas Greene. Maiden name Clarke. In the 1901 Census, found as a young servant at Dundrum House (likely the Palladian mansion in Co. Tipperary, owned by the Viscounts Hawarden). By the 1911 Census she appears as 'Bridget Clarke', age 19, single, Roman Catholic, from Dublin, working as a servant in the household of Dr. Christopher Waters (Physician & Surgeon) in Dublin. She could read and write. Married Thomas Greene after the death of his first wife Elizabeth (c.1912-1925). Mother of John, Thomas, Brendan, and Eita (Mark's grandmother-in-law). In the 1926 Census, living at York Street, Dublin. By 1951, at 38 Goldenbridge Avenue, Inchicore. Died 9 August 1970, age 78, widowed, at Adelaide Hospital, Dublin (admitted from 38 Goldenbridge Avenue). Cause of death: acute massive gangrene of small intestine and cardiac failure. Death registered 11 August 1970 by her son Brendan Greene of 62 Lenamore Drive.
sources: 1901 Census: Dundrum House, servant
1911 Census: Servant in household of Christopher Waters, Dublin
Death Certificate: Entry 22, 9 August 1970, Adelaide Hospital, Dublin
Family oral history

people.name: Thomas Greene Jr
people.notes: Eldest child of Thomas and Elizabeth (first wife).
sources: 1911 Census: Clonliffe Avenue, Mountjoy, Dublin — age 10

people.name: Patrick Greene
people.notes: Second child of Thomas and Elizabeth (first wife).
sources: 1911 Census: Clonliffe Avenue, Mountjoy, Dublin — age 8

people.name: Elizabeth Harris (née Greene)
people.notes: Daughter of Thomas and Elizabeth (first wife). Married Mr. Harris and moved to the Channel Islands. Working as a nurse during the German Occupation (1940-1945). Deported c.1942 and interned at Biberach Internment Camp (Ilag V-B), Germany. Worked for two years at the Men's Hospital under Dr. A.W.H. Donaldson. Letter of recommendation written 21 May 1945, four weeks after liberation by French forces.
sources: 1911 Census: Clonliffe Avenue, Mountjoy, Dublin — age 4
Letter of recommendation from Dr. A.W.H. Donaldson, M.B., D.P.H., Biberach Internment Camp, 21 May 1945

people.name: Mary Bridget Greene
people.notes: Daughter of Joseph Greene and Elizabeth Foran. Emigrated to the United States. If Thomas Greene is also a child of Joseph and Elizabeth Foran, Mary Bridget is Thomas's sister.
sources: Ancestry.com record: Mary Bridget Greene, b.1874, d.1966, Queens NY

people.name: William Greene
people.notes: Living in Shanderry, Portarlington North. In 1901, his wife Johanna noted 'Husband Working in Dublin' — paralleling Thomas's presence in Dublin. Married Johanna c.1888. Six children. Possible brother of Thomas Greene.
sources: 1901 Census: Shanderry, Portarlington North, King's Co. (wife Johanna as head)
1911 Census: Shanderry, Portarlington North, King's Co. — 23 yrs married, 6 born, 6 living

people.name: Dr. Christopher Waters
people.notes: In the 1911 Census, Christopher Waters (33) is head of a Dublin household. Physician and Surgeon, married 6 years to Mary A. (28, born Bolton, Lancashire, England). Two daughters: Margaret (3½) and a second daughter (2½), both born in England. The household included lodger Bridget McDonnell (22, from Limerick) and servant Bridget Clarke (19, from Dublin) — who later became Thomas Greene's second wife. This places Bridget Clarke in a professional Dublin household before her marriage to Thomas.
sources: 1911 Census: Dublin — Christopher Waters household, Form A, page 50

people.name: John Greene
people.notes: Son of Thomas Greene and Bridget Clarke (second wife). Present in the 1926 Census at York Street, Dublin. Living at 38 Goldenbridge Avenue, Inchicore, Dublin in 1951. Registered his father Thomas's death on 7 December 1951.
sources: 1926 Census: York Street, Dublin
Death Certificate of Thomas Greene, Entry 486, 6 December 1951 — informant: John Greene, Son

people.name: Thomas Greene (II)
people.notes: Son of Thomas Greene and Bridget Clarke (second wife). Named after his father. Present in the 1926 Census at York Street, Dublin. Note: Thomas also had a son Thomas Jr (b. c.1901) from his first marriage to Elizabeth.
sources: 1926 Census: York Street, Dublin
Family oral history

people.name: Brendan Greene
people.notes: Son of Thomas Greene and Bridget Clarke (second wife). Born after the 1926 Census. Living at 62 Lenamore Drive, Dublin by 1970. Registered his mother Bridget's death on 11 August 1970.
sources: Family oral history
Death Certificate of Bridget Greene, Entry 22, 9 August 1970 — informant: Brendan Greene, Son, 62 Lenamore Drive

people.name: Eita Greene
people.notes: Youngest daughter of Thomas Greene and Bridget Clarke (second wife). Mark Garrigan's grandmother-in-law. Name 'Eita' is likely an anglicised form of the Irish name Eithne. Born 1934 in Dublin.
sources: Family oral history

events.title: Earliest Record
events.description: Thos Green appears in Tithe Applotment Books, Ballyburly, King's County — approximately 8km north of Portarlington.

events.title: Thomas Greene Born
events.description: Born in Portarlington on the King's County (Offaly) side. Roman Catholic. Age 81 at death in December 1951, placing his birth at c.1870.

events.title: Mary Bridget Greene Born
events.description: Born in Portarlington, Laois. Daughter of Joseph Greene and Elizabeth Foran.

events.title: Thomas Enlists Underage
events.description: Thomas lied about his age and enlisted in the British Army at around age 14 — a practice that was remarkably common in the late Victorian army, especially among boys from poor rural backgrounds with few prospects. He likely joined the Prince of Wales's Leinster Regiment (Royal Canadians) at Birr Barracks, just 25 miles from Portarlington.

events.title: Thomas Marries Elizabeth
events.description: Thomas Greene marries Elizabeth (surname unknown) in Dublin. She is from County Dublin and approximately 7 years his senior.

events.title: Second Boer War
events.description: The Leinster Regiment served in South Africa. Thomas may have served before or during this conflict. Both the 1st and 2nd Battalions saw action at Hammonia, Harrismith, Vrede, and Bethlehem.

events.title: 1901 Census
events.description: Thomas (30) and Elizabeth (30) recorded at Ranelagh Avenue, Rathmines & Rathgar East, Dublin. Thomas is a Shop Porter. No children yet.

events.title: 1911 Census — Greene Family
events.description: Thomas (40) and Elizabeth (47) at Clonliffe Avenue, Mountjoy, Dublin. Thomas is now a Gentleman's Club Steward. Three children: Thomas Jr (10), Patrick (8), Elizabeth (4). Married 12 years. All children living.

events.title: 1911 Census — Bridget Clarke Found
events.description: In the same census, Bridget Clarke (19) appears as a servant in the Dublin household of Dr. Christopher Waters, a Physician & Surgeon from Cappawhite, Co. Limerick. Bridget is single, Roman Catholic, from Dublin, and can read and write. She will later become Thomas Greene's second wife and mother of John, Thomas, Brendan, and Eita.

events.title: Elizabeth Moves to Channel Islands
events.description: Elizabeth Greene marries Mr. Harris and moves to the Channel Islands (likely Guernsey), where she works as a nurse.

events.title: German Occupation
events.description: German forces occupy the Channel Islands on 30 June 1940. Elizabeth Harris is among approximately 66,000 civilians who remain.

events.title: Deportation
events.description: On Hitler's direct orders, approximately 2,300 Channel Islands civilians are deported to internment camps in Germany. Those born outside the islands — including Elizabeth, born in Dublin — are targeted. Elizabeth is sent to Biberach Internment Camp (Ilag V-B) in southern Germany.

events.title: Biberach Internment
events.description: Elizabeth works as a nurse at the Men's Hospital of Biberach Internment Camp under Dr. A.W.H. Donaldson, M.B., D.P.H. Despite harsh conditions and rationed food, she is described as 'keen, energetic and efficient, and always cheerful.'

events.title: Liberation
events.description: Biberach is liberated by the French First Army on 23 April 1945. On 21 May, Dr. Donaldson writes a glowing letter of recommendation for Nurse Harris, praising her nursing abilities, kindness, and even her cooking — 'her cakes and other dainties have been a god-send where the rationed food has been served up with so little of variety.'

events.title: Thomas Greene Dies
events.description: Thomas Christopher Greene dies on 6 December 1951, age 81, at 38 Goldenbridge Avenue, Inchicore, Dublin. Cause of death: carcinoma of the stomach, cachexia, cardiac failure. Still listed as a Warehouse Porter — a working man to the very end. His death was registered the following day by his son John Greene, who was living at the same address. Registrar: M. Ryan.

events.title: Bridget Greene Dies
events.description: Bridget Greene (née Clarke) dies on 9 August 1970, age 78, at Adelaide Hospital, Dublin, admitted from 38 Goldenbridge Avenue, Inchicore — still living in the family home nearly 19 years after Thomas's death. Cause of death: acute massive gangrene of small intestine and cardiac failure. Widowed. Death registered 11 August 1970 by her son Brendan Greene of 62 Lenamore Drive, Dublin.
